headings.text: Full Range of Additive Manufacturing Services

meta_description: SyBridge delivers high-quality, end-use 3D-printed parts at scale. Explore Carbon DLS, HP MJF, and Stratasys FDM for rapid prototyping and production.

sections.heading: Your High-Volume Additive Manufacturing Partner
sections.text: 3D Printing Your High-Volume Additive Manufacturing Partner Unlock advanced geometries and scale with speed, precision, and consistency. We are North America’s fastest-growing 3D printing production manufacturer, trusted by leading companies to deliver high-quality, end-use parts at scale. Backed by deep additive manufacturing expertise and significant investment in Carbon® Digital Light Synthesis, SyBridge provides the speed, precision, and consistency you need. Beyond production, we also offer rapid 3D prototyping to support every stage of your product lifecycle.

sections.heading: Full Range of Additive Manufacturing Services
sections.text: Full Range of Additive Manufacturing Services Carbon® Digital Light Synthesis HP Multi Jet Fusion™ Stratasys FDM™ High-volume production Low-volume production Production validation Rapid prototyping Elastomers Foam alternatives

sections.heading: Why Choose SyBridge for 3D Printing?
sections.text: Why Choose SyBridge for 3D Printing? Industry-leading scale: We have one of the fastest-growing additive facilities, including the #1 production capacity for high-volume DLS elastomer parts in North America Technology breadth: 3 leading 3D technologies covering a wide range of applications and geometries Wide material selection: 60+ plastic and elastomeric materials meeting demanding mechanical, thermal, and aesthetic requirements Engineering partnership: Expert support to recommend the right technologies, optimize designs, and scale efficiently to production

sections.heading: Carbon® Digital Light Synthesis (DLS)
sections.text: Carbon® Digital Light Synthesis (DLS) DLS uses digital light projection, oxygen-permeable optics, and programmable resins to rapidly produce isotropic, functional parts with excellent mechanical properties. Ideal for: High-fidelity end-use parts with complex geometries, smooth surface finishes, and injection-molding-like strength. Available Materials: Rigid: EPX 82, EPX 86 FR (flame retardant), EPX 150, FPU 50, IND 147, IND 405, MPU 100 (biocompatible), RPU 70, RPU 130, UMA 90 Non-Rigid: EPU 40, EPU 41, EPU 45, EPU 46, HDT 230, SIL 30

sections.heading: HP Multi Jet Fusion™ (MJF)
sections.text: HP Multi Jet Fusion™ (MJF) MJF builds parts by jetting agents onto a powder bed and fusing them with infrared energy. It produces durable, watertight parts with fine feature resolution and excellent detail, and can support full-color printing. Ideal for: Functional prototypes, complex assemblies, and color applications like housings, enclosures, and consumer products. Available Materials: Standard: Nylon PA-11, Nylon PA-12, PA-12 GF, PP, and TPU Color: CB Nylon PA 12

sections.heading: Stratasys Fused Deposition Modeling™ (FDM)
sections.text: Stratasys Fused Deposition Modeling™ (FDM) FDM extrudes thermoplastic filament layer by layer to build parts. Known for its material strength, low cost, and scalability, FDM is suitable for both prototyping and functional end-use parts. Ideal for: Large, strong, and cost-effective parts, especially in production-grade thermoplastics. Available Materials: ABS-ESD7, ABS-M30, ABS-M30i, Antero 800NA (PEKK), Antero840CN03 (PEKK), ASA, Nylon PA-6, Nylon PA-12, Nylon PA-12 CF (Carbon-Filled), PC-10, PC-ABS, PC-ISO, PPSF, ST-130, Ultem 1010, Ultem 9085, Ultem 9085 CG

sections.heading: Carbon® DLS: Redefining Foam Alternatives
sections.text: Carbon® DLS: Redefining Foam Alternatives Carbon’s elastomer resins provide a durable, lightweight alternative to traditional foams in sporting goods, seating, protective equipment, and more. Unlike conventional foams, elastomer DLS parts are resilient, tear-resistant, and maintain performance across millions of cycles. Additionally, with lattice design, parts can be engineered with variable stiffness, breathability, and fewer materials, eliminating multiple foams, adhesives, or assemblies. As Carbon’s largest North American partner for elastomer DLS production, SyBridge can help you move beyond traditional foam and deliver high-performance products at scale with unmatched consistency. sections.heading: Accelerate with Confidence
sections.text: Accelerate with Confidence From prototyping to production, SyBridge is your partner for additive manufacturing at scale. Whether you have questions, a new idea, or files ready to share, our team of experts is here to help. We’ll partner with you to select the right technology, optimize your designs, and deliver the highest-quality parts on your timeline. Connect with us

sections.heading: FAQs
sections.text: FAQs Do you provide design support? What are your lead times? What file formats do you accept? Can you handle validation and testing? Yes. Our team offers DfAM (Design for Additive Manufacturing) expertise to help optimize part geometry, reduce cost, and ensure the best performance in production. Production lead times vary case by case depending on material, technology, and order size. For small orders (e.g., prototyping), parts ship within 5 days of receiving your order. We typically work with STL, STEP, and IGES files, but our engineering team can accommodate most common CAD formats. Absolutely.
